artifacts.notes: rebuilt with sf_demucs.hpp session-options fix (no-cache-with-coreml, ModelCacheDirectory, RequireStaticInputShapes=1, EnableOnSubgraphs=1)
adhoc-signed with new filename to bypass amfid cache. install_name_tool retargeted the binary to @executable_path/libonnxruntime.1.24.4.v2.dylib
static library — same C ABI as before
static-shape ONNX — batch=1, channels=2, samples=343980, frames=336 baked
static-shape, 4 heads for the fine-tuned bag

runtime.notes: Warm-cache numbers are what ship in the M4L device (v2) use case where sessions outlive the split. CLI cold-run is slower for htdemucs_ft due to 4 session-setup hits; single-head variants hit the ≤12 s wall-clock target.

notes: End-to-end validated on v0/tests/fixtures/short_loop.wav. CoreML EP IS ACTIVE — ORT logs confirm 1446/1500 nodes assigned to CoreMLExecutionProvider for every Demucs head. Per-segment Demucs inference drops 4x. Cold-cache wall-clock regresses for htdemucs_ft because each of 4 heads pays ~50 s compile cost; warm-cache wall-clock for htdemucs_ft is 50 s (session-setup dominated) vs 22 s CPU baseline — known trade-off for v0 CLI, inverts for v2 M4L where sessions persist. Single-head variants already meet the target. See v0/state/A/coreml_report.md for full analysis.
